action.reasoning: Observation: LPIPS=0.6121 from avg elev/dist orbit render (n_views=11); render accepts elevation_deg+camera_distance but rejects azimuth_deg; source snippet shows internal orbit ray generation (rays_o/rays_d per view); cameras show large azimuth variance (input~45°, targets~230° etc.) while elev~ -60° consistent. Analysis: Poor score from pose mismatch—default orbit (likely az starting at 0°) doesn't align to dataset's specific target azimuths relative to input view (model treats input as canonical front). Strong zero-shot TripoSR baseline but needs exact per-target camera alignment for big LPIPS drop. Decision: Enhance inspect_render.py to dump full render signature+params+complete source (reveals accepted args & camera calc), test many likely azimuth-controlling kwargs (azimuth, azimuths, start_azimuth, orbit params, lists for multi-view) with relative_az=(target_az-input_az)%360 on n_views=1, identify working API, then update predict.py to loop over each of 11 exact target cameras for pose-matched renders. Test on val subset before full submit_eval. No training yet (respects size, time).

=== Full source of model.render ===
    def render(
        self,
        scene_codes,
        n_views: int,
        elevation_deg: float = 0.0,
        camera_distance: float = 1.9,
        fovy_deg: float = 40.0,
        height: int = 256,
        width: int = 256,
        return_type: str = "pil",
    ):
        rays_o, rays_d = get_spherical_cameras(
            n_views, elevation_deg, camera_distance, fovy_deg, height, width
        )
        rays_o, rays_d = rays_o.to(scene_codes.device), rays_d.to(scene_codes.device)

        def process_output(image: torch.FloatTensor):
            if return_type == "pt":
                return image
            elif return_type == "np":
                return image.detach().cpu().numpy()
            elif return_type == "pil":
                return Image.fromarray(
                    (image.detach().cpu().numpy() * 255.0).astype(np.uint8)
                )
            else:
                raise NotImplementedError

        images = []
        for scene_code in scene_codes:
            images_ = []
            for i in range(n_views):
                with torch.no_grad():
                    image = self.renderer(
                        self.decoder, scene_code, rays_o[i], rays_d[i]
                    )
                images_.append(process_output(image))
            images.append(images_)

        return images
